JAVA:
```javascript
## 2026-03-25
# File / exe

- bin Folder : directory containing executable files
- javac.exe : Java compiler (used to compile source code)
- java.exe : Java runtime (used to execute compiled programs) 

# Java Platform summary
1) Java SE (Standard Edition) 
		- The basic Java Platform
		- Can be set up by installing the JDK
2) Java EE (Enterprise Edition)
	  - Web Development (servlet / JSP)
3) Java ME (Micro Edition)
		- Designed for mobile enviorments

# Java compressed file format

1) jar -> *.jar, class file compressed
2) war -> *.war, web archive, web application compressed
3) ear -> *.ear, enterprise archive, .jar + .war

# Java write and run

1) source code
2) compile (use compiler)
	- javac Test.java ---> Test.class create
3) execution (use interpreter)

# JDK and JRE
1) JDK (Java Development Kit)
	- Include utilities : `javac.exe`, `javadoc.exe`, `jar.exe`, etc 
	- Provides tools to convert `.java` → `.class` (bytecode) 

2) JRE (Java Runtime Environment)
	- Client-side enviorment capable of running application only
	- JVM + Standard libraries

3) JVM (Java Virtual Machine)
	- Interpreter and executes Bytecode('.class') according to the OS & H/W
	- → The core of "Write Once, Run Anywhere" 

4) Layer Structure
> JDK ⊃ JRE ⊃ JVM ⊃ OS ⊃ H/W

# Identifier
1) System-defined Identifier
	- Cannot be changed 
	
2) User-defined Identifier
	- ❌ Cannot use reserved words
	- ⚠️ Case-sensitive
	
# Class Name
	- First letter must be uppercase
	- Use meaningful nouns 
	- Must match the filename exactly
	- After the first letter: lowercase letters, numbers, `_`, `$` allowed
	
# Main method
	- Entry point when running java program (starting point)
	- Every application must exactly one main method
	
# Java API
	- A class file that can help developers when developing applications

# format type
	1) %s : string (" ") 
	2) %c : char (' ')
	3) %d : integer
	4) %f : float
	5) %b : boolean
```

```javascript
## 2026-03-27

# Java Data Types 
# Primitive Data type

1) Integer 
	- byte : 1byte (8bit)
	- short : 2byte (16bit)
	- int : 4byte (32bit)
	- long : 8byte (64bit)
	* Number system : binery (0b1), octal (01), hexadecimal (0x1)
	* Exceeds int range : muse add L at the end
	
2) Floating Point
	- float : 4byte (32bit) / must add F at the end
	- double : 8byte (64bit) / D is optional
	
3) Character
	- char : 2byte (16bit)
	* Must use single quotes -> 'A', 'M'
	* For strings, use string class
	
4) Boolean
	- boolean : 1byte (8bit) / only true or false
	
5) Escape Characters
	- \n : New line = enter
	- \t : Tab
	- \" : print "
	- \' : print '
	- \\ : print \
	

# Reference Data Type (Non-Primitive Data Type )
```

```javascript
## 2026-03-30

# Variable
 - int num                        // Declaration
 - num = 10;                      // Initialized
 - System.out.println(num + 10);  // Use
 - num = 20;                      // Change
 
# Primivite vs Reference
 - Example : int num = 10  /  String name = "홍길동"
 - Stores : Actual value 10 / Memory address
 - Size : Varies by type / Always 4 bytes
 
# Local variable
 - stored in Stack memory
 - Must be initialized before use
 - Created and deleted every time the method is called
 - Created when method runs, deleted when it ends
 
# instance variable
 - Stored in Heap memory
 - Automatically initialized with default values
 - Created separately for each new
 - Created on new, deleted by GC when unrefereced
 
# Stack vs Heap
 - Stores : Local variable  /  Actual object 
 - Management : Auto  /  Managed by GC
 - Size/speed : small and fast / Large and slow
 
# new Object Create
 - Created object in Heap and returns its address
 - Every new created a separate object
 - If unreferenced, becomes a GC target
 - null = pointing to nothing

# Constant
 - Value cannot be changed
 - Uses "final" keyword
 - Written in UPPERCASE
 
# Automatic Type Conversion
 - byte -> short -> int -> long -> float -> double
 - Rule.
	 1. Operation result of types smaller than int returns as int
	 2. Small type + Large type = returns as Large type
	 
# Operator
 - Arithmetic : +, -, *, %, /
 - Assignment = (a += b) == (a = a+b)
 - Comparison = Result is always boolean
 - Logical &&, ||, ! Result is always boolean
 
# Increment / Decrement Operator
 - int a = 5 / int b = ++a, a++
 - Used alone, pre/post result is the same
 - Used with otehr operators, result differs
 
# Ternary Operator
 - variable = (condition) ? true : false
 
# Scanner
 - import java.util.Scanner; // import
 - Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in); // Create Object
 - int n = scan.nextInt(); // Integer input
 - scan.close(); // Release resource
 - System.in = keyboard / System.out = Monitor
 

* separately : 별도
  declaration : 선언
```
Git: > Working Directory → git add → Staging Area → git commit → local repository → git push → Remote Repository

```javascript
git init : Create local and remote repository
git add : Create and add files to track in local repository
git commit + message : Create version of tracked files
git commit + --amend : You can modify immediately after committing
git log : Check version history
git diff : Compare between versions
git reset, revert : Revert to a specific version
git branch (name): Create new branch 
git merge : Merge branches
git push : Push the local branch to remote repository
git status : Check repository status 
git restore : Unstage the staged files
git remote : Connecting remote repository
git push : 
git pull : It is similar to fetch, but automatically merges after fetching
git fetch : Fetch the latest changes from the remote repo without merging them into the local branch
git clone : Copy remote repo to local and set up automatically (Copy only Main)

# git add + commit (The file must have been tracked at least once)
	-> git commit -a m "message"
		 git commit -am "message"
		 
# git log --oneline : one commit per line
	git log --oneline --graph --all : Check the branching point
	git log -p : Print changes
	git log --relative-date : Print elapsed time since commit based on current time

# git restore : Delete the modified content
  git restore --staged : Cancel add
  
# git diff : Compare working directory and repository
	git diff --staged : Compare staging area and repository
	git diff HEAD : git diff + diff --staged
	git diff Hash1 Hash2 : Compare between commits
	
# git reset --soft <commit id / HEAD^> : Cancel commit
	git reset --mixed <commit id> : Cancel add and commit
	git reset --hard <commit id> : Cancel add, commit and delete modified content
	
# git branch -m/M (name) : Change branch name
		-m : Normal rename / -M : Force rename (overwrite)
  git branch -D (name) : Delete branch
  git branch -r : Watch remote branch
  git branch -a : Watch local and remote branch
  
# git switch (name) : Change branch
  git switch -c (name) : Create and change branch
  
# git merge -D (name) : Delete branch after merge
	git merge-base main (name) : Check the branching point of a specific branch
	
# git remote add origin (repo address) : Connecting remote repo
	git remote : View remote repo aliases
	git remote rm origin : Disconnect remote
	
# git push origin --delete (branch) : Delete branch 

# git clone (address) : Clone remote repo to local
	git clone (address) (folder name) : Clone + custom foler name
	git clone -b (branch) (address) :
	git clone --depth 1 (address) :
```

```javascript
# branch
1. Fast-Forward (merge commit X)
 - history가 일직선으로 이어지며, 별도 커밋 없이 포인터만 앞으로 이동한다. 분기했을 때 기준으로 main에 변경사항이 없으면 사용한다.

2. 3-Way Merge (merge commit O)
 - 브랜치를 병합하면 history에 병합기록이 남으며 부모가 2개 생성된다.
 
3. Rebase (merge commit X)
 - Fast-Forward와 거의 일치하지만 분기했을 때 기준으로 main에 변경사항이 생겼을 때 사용한다. 
 

```

```javascript
.git/refs/heads/main
-> main branch의 최신 commit 정보가 저장 (.git/HEAD가 참조)

.git/ogjects
-> 해시값에 해당되는 40글자 중 2글자에 해당하는 폴더에 나머지 38글자를 저장해 
	 해시값을 관리한다.
```

```javascript
# gitignore : git이 추적하지 않을 파일/폴더를 지정 (git add . 무시)
	git rm --cached secrets2.txt : 이미 tracked 된 파일 (remove cache)
	
# rekect error : local 과 remote histroy가 달라서 push가 거부할때 발생
resolve -> 원격을 가져와서 병합 후 충돌 있으면 해결, 다시 push 

```
